bugs.description: Parameter 'limit' is declared as optional (?:) but is used in a strict equality comparison without handling the undefined case. When limit is undefined, the expression 'cnt === limit' becomes 'number === number | undefined', which TypeScript flags as an error.
bugs.location: Function parameter 'limit?: number' and usage in 'cnt === limit' comparison

bugs.description: Variable 'texts' is typed as a tuple '[ChunkSlice<T>, ...ChunkSlice<T>[]]' instead of an array. Tuples do not have a 'push' method, so calling 'texts.push()' causes a compile-time error.
bugs.location: Variable declaration 'const texts: [ChunkSlice<T>, ...ChunkSlice<T>[]]' and method call 'texts.push(...)'

bugs.description: The callback function's return type is explicitly annotated as '[boolean, void]' (a tuple type), but the overlay.chunkSlices0 method expects a return type of 'boolean | void'. TypeScript reports that the return type is not assignable to the expected type.
bugs.location: Callback function return type annotation '(chunk, off, len): [boolean, void]'

expectedErrors: TS2365: Operator '===' cannot be applied to types 'number' and 'number | undefined'.; TS2339: Property 'push' does not exist on type '[ChunkSlice<T>, ...ChunkSlice<T>[]]'.; TS2354: Type '[boolean, void]' is not assignable to type 'boolean | void'.
